instance_reference.note: Per-accelerator-hour figures derived from the whole-instance hourly list price divided by 8 GPUs.

price_note: LIST PRICES only, as published or derived from public vendor price pages; negotiated enterprise discounts and neocloud offers (B200 on-demand runs ~$3.70-7.00/GPU-hr at neoclouds as of mid-2026) are NOT reflected. The reserved_1y figure is the AWS EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML per-accelerator rate (US regions, effective 2026-07-01) used as the reserved-capacity reference because AWS does not publish a standard 1-year RI rate for p6-b200; Capacity Block pricing is revised quarterly. Spot prices are dynamic snapshots with inconsistent availability.
energy_note: Electricity sub-term uses assumed_avg_utilization x tdp_watts x assumed_pue x electricity_usd_per_kwh. utilization, PUE (AWS does not publish fleet PUE; 1.15 is an assumption) and the $0.10/kWh industrial-power ballpark are ASSUMPTIONS, not measurements.

prices.derivation: AWS EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML, P6-B200 US-region rate per accelerator-hour, effective 2026-07-01 (next scheduled revision Oct 2026). Reserved-capacity product; NOT a standard 1-year RI rate (none is published for p6-b200).
p6-b200 spot snapshot ~$21.61/hr per node / 8 GPUs (~81% below on-demand); availability is inconsistent in practice

tdp.note: NVIDIA HGX B200 Total Graphics Power 1000W (OEM product guide reproducing the NVIDIA specification).

pue.note: ASSUMPTION. AWS does not publish fleet-average PUE.
